title: Rich Intense Algerian Tripe Tagine
description: A hearty, energizing stew originating from Algeria, made with tender tripe, kombu-cold-extract, and a blend of aromatic spices.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the beef tripe and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Add the kombu-cold-extract, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover and transfer to the oven.
Bake at 300°F (150°C) for 2 hours, or until the tripe is very tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
